title: Secret-coverage DNA + source ingestion. A new stack-secrets extension that ingests a secret source (directory OR zip, default .agentspace/secrets) and a secret-coverage DNA that lists + keeps-listed the required secret KEY each repo of a stack needs — values-blind throughout. Folds in the check/measure scorer (the natural pairing with the DNA, M27-D3.2 Fate-1); provision + pre-flight wiring + demo-aware scoring stay M28.
position_note: M27 is the 1st of 4 milestones of v1.6 'stage door' (M27->M30, strictly sequential). M27 = the DNA + the ingestion reader + the scorer/diff verbs; M28 = the provision engine + the coverage/verify gate + bring-up wiring; M29 = the corpus doc (secrets-spec.md) + the /stack-secrets skill + CLAUDE.md wiring + setup_guide.md TODO retirement; M30 = the build-from-stack-dev field-bake. After this close: build M28.
kind: an ext-code-bearing section milestone. The new stack-secrets section (~5180 lines: 11 source + 11 test files + the committed 55-gene secret-dna.json + the section README) lives ENTIRELY in the rosetta-extensions authoring copy, on branch m27/secret-coverage-dna, tagged stage-door-m27 @ 195ef93. The rosetta m27/secret-coverage-dna branch is markdown/text only (the milestone planning docs: overview/progress/decisions/spec-notes/kb-fidelity-audit + the close records). close-milestone's merge is entirely rosetta-side; the ext side (main merge / tag) is finalized by the orchestrator.

sections.list: stack-secrets scaffold (go.mod + cmd/stacksecrets) · source reader dir mode (values-blind) · source reader zip mode · source-dir layout contract (zEnvs/per-repo .env never silently ingested) · secret-DNA schema + secret-dna.json (gene = repo x KEY; strict load + Validate) · introspect from the hybrid source · list verb · diff verb (keep-listed gate exit 1 + undeclared-runtime-required guard) · waived classes modeled (AWS-mount, profile-gated, optional Bunny/GCloud) · alias families encoded (GH_PAT family) vs distinct-similar (OPENAI_KEY/OPENAI_API_KEY not auto-aliased) · hermetic unit tests (no values) · ext tag stage-door-m27

field_bugs_surfaced.note: No live-field bugs at M27 (the field-bake is M30). The build's diff-vs-stack-dev DID catch 10 real cross-repo DNA omissions in the hand-curated map (tracked secrets declared for a repo with no gene there) — all fixed Fate-1 during build by adding the genuinely-needed genes (the keep-listed gate earning its keep against the real stack-dev). The harden pass surfaced 0 bugs (the build code held under deepening). The close review surfaced 0 code bugs — 2 ext doc-hygiene findings only.

tests.language: Go (stdlib-only — the secret-DNA is JSON, the readers are archive/zip + bufio; no go.sum). All three packages co-locate tests with source; no source file lacks a test.
tests.go_total_note: The v1.5-baseline 867 (alignment 52 + clerkenstein 223 + stack-seeding 259 + stack-snapshot 333) is unchanged — M27 touches no prior section. The +113 is entirely the new stack-secrets section (build + a two-pass harden that added 40: 27 Pass 1 + 13 Pass 2). Measured at ext m27 head (Test+Fuzz func-decls, full --count=1).
tests.python_note: Python total (459 collected: 451 active + 8 env-gated skip) unchanged — M27 touches no Python (stack-secrets is Go-only; the rosetta branch is markdown-only).
tests.flake_gate: 5/5 sequential -shuffle clean runs of the full stack-secrets suite at close (post-fix). -race + gofmt + go vet all clean.
tests.rosetta_test_note: The rosetta m27 branch carries no executable code (planning + close-record markdown only). The executable test surface is the ext stack-secrets section, counted at the ext-repo level.

coverage.note: From the harden pass (recorded in progress.md § M27: Hardening). Statements total 93.6% -> 98.3% (+4.7). By package: cmd/stacksecrets 87.4% -> 97.9% (+10.5); secretdna 96.9% -> 99.2% (+2.3); source 91.0% -> 96.2% (+5.2). The remaining uncovered lines are near-impossible defensive branches (main()'s os.Exit wrapper, post-successful-read f.Close() error returns, a json.MarshalIndent failure on a valid struct) — deliberately not chased with contrived harnesses (coverage-is-a-finder).
coverage.harden_bugs_note: 0 bugs in the harden pass — the build-phase code was solid; every new test passed against the shipped implementation (the layout contract, values-blind extraction, the two-tier diff gate, the fail-loud error paths all held under adversarial input).

benchmarks.note: No performance-sensitive code in M27 (a values-blind DNA harness over .env-shaped files: JSON load + bufio scans + map lookups; no hot path, no perf surface). No threshold tests warranted.

review.findings_note: 2 findings, both Fate-1 ext doc-hygiene: 0 scope · 0 code-quality · 1 docs (DOC-1: stack-secrets missing from the ext top-level README Sections table) · 1 tests (TEST-1: the section README quoted a stale '94 tests'; ground truth 113) · 0 decision-blend (M27 corpus doc is M29 scope per the repo-split). Both fixed in the ext commit 537aeff. 0 escape-hatch.
review.scope: GREEN — all 12 deliverable boxes checked; the overview In: list fully delivered; the Out items (provision/gate/wiring -> M28, skill+doc -> M29, field-bake -> M30) correctly belong elsewhere. The check/measure fold is the documented Fate-1 (M27-D3.2), not creep — withholding the scorer would have been a partial landing (the DNA would have no way to be exercised). No silent drops; no code TODO/FIXME/HACK in the diff.
review.code_quality: GREEN — consistent patterns across all 11 source files (uniform error handling, naming, values-blind discipline); go vet + gofmt clean; no dead code (sortedKeys is a test-consumed Source-impl helper). The values-blind invariant verified end-to-end: only ClassifyShape reads a value (as a discarded local), the committed secret-dna.json carries zero secret-shaped tokens, and the single value-reading call site (source.go:310) immediately discards into ClassifyShape.
review.adversarial: GREEN — 4 scenarios recorded in decisions.md § Adversarial review (value leak through a shape probe / error message; zEnvs-or-stray-.env silent ingestion; malformed/oversized line crash-or-false-clean; vacuously-green gate). Each was already turned into a regression test by the harden pass; close confirmed the coverage, no new code fix.
review.docs: GREEN after fix — DOC-1 (the stack-secrets section row was absent from the ext README Sections table) added; the section README is otherwise accurate (layout contract, hybrid introspect, the two-tier gate, the gene schema, alias/waived classes all match the code). No rosetta corpus doc is due at M27 (confirmed by grep: 0 stack-secrets references in corpus/CLAUDE.md/.claude — the user-facing doc lands M29 per the repo-split).
review.tests: GREEN after fix — TEST-1 (handbook count drift 94 -> 113) reconciled to runner truth. Full suite -race green (113 funcs), flake gate 5/5. No test gaps (the harden pass already covered the risk surface: layout contract, values-blind extraction, the two-tier gate, alias vs distinct-similar, fail-loud error paths).
review.decision_triage: M27-D1 (the M26->M27 renumber) / M27-D2 (the DNA-scoped two-tier gate) / M27-D3 (stdlib-only + check/measure fold + introspect read-only + profile scoping + encrypted-zip boundary) -> ARCHIVE (maintainer-only). The M28/M29 handoffs (provision must defer to the DIRECTUS_TOKEN strip; the conditional per-gene-profile residual; the encrypted-zip boundary) are already captured in the decision text and the spec-notes; the user/developer-facing corpus doc for the feature is M29 scope per the repo-split. No M27 KB blend.
review.deferral_audit: GREEN — audit-deferrals/deferral-audit-2026-06-14-m27-close.md. M27 is the first milestone of v1.6, so there is no within-release item to repeat. 2 milestone-scoped items fated: DEF-M27-01 (encrypted-zip age/gpg) -> DROP (a documented v1 scope boundary in M27-D3.5, not a pending deferral — a complete landing pulls in a crypto + key-management surface contradicting the deliberate stdlib-only values-blind design); DEF-M27-02 (per-gene profile tag) -> LAND-NEXT Fate-2 (M28-owned-if-it-wires-non-default-profile-bring-ups; already captured in M27-D3.4, no M28 overview.md edit required). 3 inherited release-level backlog items (DEF-M10-01 S3 blob bytes, DEF-M21-01, M25-D9) -> KEEP-DEFERRED, re-signed <1 day ago at the v1.5 close, all orthogonal to secret provisioning. 0 repeat, 0 chronic, 0 aged-out, 0 blockers.

notes: M27 is the 1st of 4 milestones of v1.6 'stage door' — the secret-provisioning release's foundation: a values-blind secret-coverage DNA (55 genes / 6 repos) + a DNA-driven dir/zip source reader that structurally can't ingest a zEnvs backup mirror or a stray .env, plus the check/measure scorer and the DNA-scoped two-tier keep-listed diff gate folded in. The whole section is stdlib-only (no go.sum) so the values-blind audit surface is trivially small. Verified live against the real stack-dev: diff exits 0 (0 gate-fatal), check reports real coverage + per-repo shortfalls with NO secret value printed. Close was clean: 2 findings (0 scope / 0 code / 1 docs / 1 tests / 0 blend), both Fate-1 ext doc-hygiene fixed in 537aeff; 4 adversarial scenarios recorded (all already test-pinned); the deferral audit GREEN (DEF-M27-01 DROP as a v1 boundary, DEF-M27-02 Fate-2 M28-owned, inherited 3 KEEP); 0 escape-hatch. Go 867 -> 980 (+113); Python 459 unchanged; flake 0; 5/5 -shuffle clean; -race + gofmt + go vet clean. The rosetta merge is markdown/text only (the code is in ext, tagged stage-door-m27 @ 195ef93). Next: build M28 (the provision engine + the coverage/verify gate).
